rationale.R1.2: Reviewing the section involves verifying the logical consistency of the arguments about the system's approach and guarantees.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation requires checking if the claims about latencies and guarantees are aligned with the reported experiments.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing the novelty of ConsistencyChain compared to recent work is a central part of reviewing this section.
rationale.R1.7: Evaluating the accuracy and coverage of references to prior work and internal sections is expected in the review.
rationale.RX.1: The review should include checking for proper disclosure of uncertainties in the protocol and experiments.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims in the guarantees and performance reports is central to the review.
rationale.RX.3: Ensuring the section adheres to its scope on consistency in geo-distributed settings is important.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream harm avoidance is not specifically requested for this technical section on distributed systems.
rationale.RX.5: Verifying provenance and traceability of the claims and experimental results is part of the review.
